aids.title: y = mx + c (School Version)
aids.content: Linear regression is just 'y = mx + c' from school! In ML: y = wx + b (w = weight/slope, b = bias/intercept). For multiple features: y = w1x1 + w2x2 + ... + b

aids.title: L1 = Lasso = Sparse, L2 = Ridge = Shrink
aids.content: L1 (Lasso) makes coefficients exactly Zero (Z in LaZZo). L2 (Ridge) Reduces all coefficients (R in Ridge). ElasticNet = Both combined.

aids.title: Gradient Descent = Hiking Down a Mountain
aids.content: Gradient descent is like hiking down a foggy mountain to reach the valley (minimum). At each step, you feel the slope (gradient) and move in the steepest downhill direction (negative gradient). Learning rate = step size.
